raw: {"confidence":0.99,"critical_failures":{"A":[],"B":["incoherent_block_arc"]},"generic_mechanism":"Belief change accumulates when causal explanations precede and support reframing; reversing that dependency turns earned conclusions into disconnected claims and forces readers to reconstruct the intervention themselves.","paraphrased_evidence":{"A":"The chapters progressively recast consumption as withdrawal relief, conditioned preference, and failed satisfaction, then portray abstinence as restored choice; sweeping claims weaken credibility but the intervention remains intelligible.","B":"Most of the same reasoning appears in reverse or fragmented order, placing summaries and conclusions before their foundations, breaking local transitions and preventing the three chapters from building persuasive momentum."},"product_parity_verdict":"A","scores":{"belief_movement":{"A":7,"B":3},"benefit_dismantling":{"A":7,"B":3},"cumulative_progression":{"A":7,"B":1},"escape_conviction":{"A":7,"B":3}}}

parsed.generic_mechanism: Belief change accumulates when causal explanations precede and support reframing; reversing that dependency turns earned conclusions into disconnected claims and forces readers to reconstruct the intervention themselves.

paraphrased_evidence.A: The chapters progressively recast consumption as withdrawal relief, conditioned preference, and failed satisfaction, then portray abstinence as restored choice; sweeping claims weaken credibility but the intervention remains intelligible.
paraphrased_evidence.B: Most of the same reasoning appears in reverse or fragmented order, placing summaries and conclusions before their foundations, breaking local transitions and preventing the three chapters from building persuasive momentum.

paraphrased_evidence.ours: Most of the same reasoning appears in reverse or fragmented order, placing summaries and conclusions before their foundations, breaking local transitions and preventing the three chapters from building persuasive momentum.
paraphrased_evidence.ref: The chapters progressively recast consumption as withdrawal relief, conditioned preference, and failed satisfaction, then portray abstinence as restored choice; sweeping claims weaken credibility but the intervention remains intelligible.
